crime: She dropped the act exactly once, and said across the table that she was not all right. The girl there slid her a chocolate bar and went back to her magazine.
Her parents had done the same all her life: every need she voiced came back as something bought. No one listened.
She chose the shower hour, stoppered and filled the wash-basin, and held the girl's head under — laughing softly the whole time. A laughing throat cannot scream; the girl thus drowned.
Meanwhile her phone played in the lounge, running a set billed as live — forty minutes of her routines, recorded the week before.
That evening, the girls waiting their turn in the shower room's changing area laughed at nothing until it hurt. In the lounge, one girl in a foul mood sat through the whole set without laughing once.
A recorded laugh is only a sound; it compels no one. The set with the unlaughing listener was the recording.
fields.epitaph: Everything they ever handed her was wrapped.
fields.epithet: The Clown Paid to Go Away
execution: She was seated on a single chair at the center of the execution stand, the prisoners ringed around it.
A machine was wheeled in that dispenses parcels, wrapped and ribboned, one every few seconds — at her feet, on her lap, on her shoulders, faster than she could push them away.
The machine stopped when the pile stopped moving.
fields.magic: When she laughs, everyone who hears her laughs with her until she stops.
